   108→class TriValue:
   109→    """3-valued logic value using two masks:
   110→    - known_mask: 1 = bit is known, 0 = bit is unknown (X)
   111→    - value_mask: the value for known bits (meaningful only where known_mask bit is 1)
   112→    """
   113→
   114→    def __init__(self, value_mask: int = 0, known_mask: int = None, width: int = 1):
   115→        self.value_mask = value_mask
   116→        self.known_mask = (1 << width) - 1 if known_mask is None else known_mask
   117→        self.width = width
   118→
   119→    @property
   120→    def is_fully_known(self) -> bool:
   121→        return self.known_mask == (1 << self.width) - 1
   122→
   123→    @property
   124→    def has_unknown(self) -> bool:
   125→        return self.known_mask != (1 << self.width) - 1
   126→
   127→    def get_bit(self, bit: int) -> str:
   128→        if not (self.known_mask >> bit) & 1:
   129→            return 'X'
   130→        return '1' if (self.value_mask >> bit) & 1 else '0'
   131→
   132→    def to_int(self) -> int:
   133→        if self.has_unknown:
   134→            raise ValueError("Cannot convert TriValue with unknown bits to int")
   135→        return self.value_mask
   136→
   137→    def __repr__(self):
   138→        return f"TriValue(value={self.value_mask}, known={self.known_mask}, width={self.width})"
   139→
   140→    @staticmethod
   141→    def from_int(value: int, width: int) -> 'TriValue':
   142→        return TriValue(value, (1 << width) - 1, width)
   143→
   144→    @staticmethod
   145→    def from_bit(bit: str, width: int = 1) -> 'TriValue':
   146→        if bit.upper() == 'X':
   147→            return TriValue(0, 0, width)
   148→        return TriValue(int(bit), 1, width)
   149→
   150→    def copy(self) -> 'TriValue':
   151→        return TriValue(self.value_mask, self.known_mask, self.width)
   152→
   153→    def with_width(self, width: int) -> 'TriValue':
   154→        if width == self.width:
   155→            return self.copy()
   156→        clip = (1 << min(width, self.width)) - 1
   157→        return TriValue(self.value_mask & clip, self.known_mask & clip, width)
   158→
   159→    @staticmethod
   160→    def not_(a: 'TriValue') -> 'TriValue':
   161→        return TriValue((~a.value_mask) & a.known_mask, a.known_mask, a.width)
   162→
   163→    @staticmethod
   164→    def and2(a: 'TriValue', b: 'TriValue') -> 'TriValue':
   165→        a_known0 = a.known_mask & ~a.value_mask
   166→        b_known0 = b.known_mask & ~b.value_mask
   167→        result_known0 = a_known0 | b_known0
   168→
   169→        a_known1 = a.known_mask & a.value_mask
   170→        b_known1 = b.known_mask & b.value_mask
   171→        result_known1 = a_known1 & b_known1
   172→
   173→        return TriValue(result_known1, result_known0 | result_known1, a.width)
   174→
   175→    @staticmethod
   176→    def or2(a: 'TriValue', b: 'TriValue') -> 'TriValue':
   177→        a_known1 = a.known_mask & a.value_mask
   178→        b_known1 = b.known_mask & b.value_mask
   179→        result_known1 = a_known1 | b_known1
   180→
   181→        a_known0 = a.known_mask & ~a.value_mask
   182→        b_known0 = b.known_mask & ~b.value_mask
   183→        result_known0 = a_known0 & b_known0
   184→
   185→        return TriValue(result_known1, result_known0 | result_known1, a.width)
   186→
   187→    @staticmethod
   188→    def xor2(a: 'TriValue', b: 'TriValue') -> 'TriValue':
   189→        both_known = a.known_mask & b.known_mask
   190→        return TriValue((a.value_mask ^ b.value_mask) & both_known, both_known, a.width)
   191→
   192→    @staticmethod
   193→    def mux(sel: 'TriValue', a: 'TriValue', b: 'TriValue') -> 'TriValue':
   194→        if sel.known_mask & 1:
   195→            return a.copy() if sel.value_mask & 1 else b.copy()
   196→
   197→        both_known = a.known_mask & b.known_mask
   198→        same_value = both_known & ~(a.value_mask ^ b.value_mask)
   199→        return TriValue((a.value_mask | b.value_mask) & same_value, same_value, a.width)
   200→
   201→    @staticmethod
   202→    def eq(a: 'TriValue', b: 'TriValue') -> 'TriValue':
   203→        mask = (1 << a.width) - 1
   204→        both_known = a.known_mask & b.known_mask
   205→
   206→        diff_bits = both_known & (a.value_mask ^ b.value_mask)
   207→        if diff_bits:
   208→            return TriValue(0, 1, 1)
   209→
   210→        if both_known == mask and not (a.value_mask ^ b.value_mask):
   211→            return TriValue(1, 1, 1)
   212→
   213→        return TriValue(0, 0, 1)
   214→
   215→    @staticmethod
   216→    def reduce_and(a: 'TriValue') -> 'TriValue':
   217→        mask = (1 << a.width) - 1
   218→        if a.known_mask & ~a.value_mask:
   219→            return TriValue(0, 1, 1)
   220→        if a.known_mask == mask and (a.value_mask & mask) == mask:
   221→            return TriValue(1, 1, 1)
   222→        return TriValue(0, 0, 1)
   223→
   224→    @staticmethod
   225→    def reduce_or(a: 'TriValue') -> 'TriValue':
   226→        mask = (1 << a.width) - 1
   227→        if a.known_mask & a.value_mask:
   228→            return TriValue(1, 1, 1)
   229→        if a.known_mask == mask and (a.value_mask & mask) == 0:
   230→            return TriValue(0, 1, 1)
   231→        return TriValue(0, 0, 1)
   232→
   233→    @staticmethod
   234→    def reduce_xor(a: 'TriValue') -> 'TriValue':
   235→        mask = (1 << a.width) - 1
   236→        if a.known_mask != mask:
   237→            return TriValue(0, 0, 1)
   238→        return TriValue(bin(a.value_mask & mask).count('1') % 2, 1, 1)

   251→_BINARY_PROPAGATE = frozenset({'AND', 'OR', 'XOR', 'NAND', 'NOR', 'XNOR'})
   252→_SELECT = frozenset({'MUX', 'ITE'})
   253→_REDUCE = frozenset({'REDUCE_AND', 'REDUCE_OR', 'REDUCE_XOR'})

   263→def _all_signal_widths(circuit) -> dict:
   264→    return {sig.name: sig.width for sig in circuit.inputs + circuit.outputs + circuit.wires}

   266→
   267→def _expr_width(expr, signal_widths: dict) -> int:
   268→    if isinstance(expr, Literal):
   269→        return expr.width
   270→    if isinstance(expr, Identifier):
   271→        return signal_widths[expr.name]
   272→    if isinstance(expr, Index):
   273→        return 1
   274→    if isinstance(expr, Slice):
   275→        return expr.hi - expr.lo + 1
   276→    if isinstance(expr, Concat):
   277→        return sum(_expr_width(p, signal_widths) for p in expr.parts)
   278→    if isinstance(expr, Call):
   279→        op = expr.operator
   280→        if op in _UNARY_PROPAGATE | _BINARY_PROPAGATE:
   281→            return _expr_width(expr.args[0], signal_widths)
   282→        if op in _SELECT:
   283→            return _expr_width(expr.args[1], signal_widths)
   284→        if op in _REDUCE | {'EQ'}:
   285→            return 1
   286→        raise RuntimeError(f"Unknown operator: {op}")
   287→    raise RuntimeError(f"Unknown expression type: {type(expr)}")
   288→

   290→def _collect_dependencies(expr) -> set:
   291→    if isinstance(expr, Identifier):
   292→        return {expr.name}
   293→    if isinstance(expr, (Index, Slice)):
   294→        return _collect_dependencies(expr.expr)
   295→    if isinstance(expr, Concat):
   296→        deps = set()
   297→        for part in expr.parts:
   298→            deps.update(_collect_dependencies(part))
   299→        return deps
   300→    if isinstance(expr, Call):
   301→        deps = set()
   302→        for arg in expr.args:
   303→            deps.update(_collect_dependencies(arg))
   304→        return deps
   305→    return set()

   308→def parse_literal(text: str, filename: str = None, line: int = None, col: int = None) -> Tuple[int, int]:
   309→    original = text
   310→    text = text.replace('_', '')
   311→
   312→    if text == 'X':
   313→        raise CircError("CircParseError", "Literal 'X' values are not allowed in .circ files", filename, line, col)
   314→
   315→    if text in ('0', '1'):
   316→        return int(text), 1
   317→
   318→    if text.lower().startswith('0b'):
   319→        digits = text[2:]
   320→        if not digits:
   321→            raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid binary literal: {original}", filename, line, col)
   322→        if not re.match(r'^[01]+$', digits):
   323→            raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid binary literal: {original}", filename, line, col)
   324→        return int(digits, 2), len(digits)
   325→
   326→    if text.lower().startswith('0x'):
   327→        digits = text[2:]
   328→        if not digits:
   329→            raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid hex literal: {original}", filename, line, col)
   330→        if not re.match(r'^[0-9a-fA-F]+$', digits):
   331→            raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid hex literal: {original}", filename, line, col)
   332→        value = int(digits, 16)
   333→        return value, len(digits) * 4
   334→
   335→    sized_match = re.match(r"^(\d+)'([bhd])([0-9a-fA-F_]+)$", text, re.IGNORECASE)
   336→    if sized_match:
   337→        width = int(sized_match.group(1))
   338→        base = sized_match.group(2).lower()
   339→        digits = sized_match.group(3).replace('_', '')
   340→
   341→        if width <= 0:
   342→            raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid literal width: {original}", filename, line, col)
   343→
   344→        base_validators = {
   345→            'b': (r'^[01]+$', 2, f"Invalid binary literal: {original}"),
   346→            'h': (r'^[0-9a-fA-F]+$', 16, f"Invalid hex literal: {original}"),
   347→            'd': (r'^[0-9]+$', 10, f"Invalid decimal literal: {original}"),
   348→        }
   349→        pattern, base_int, err_msg = base_validators[base]
   350→        if not re.match(pattern, digits):
   351→            raise CircError("CircParseError", err_msg, filename, line, col)
   352→        try:
   353→            value = int(digits, base_int)
   354→        except ValueError:
   355→            raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid literal: {original}", filename, line, col)
   356→
   357→        if value > (1 << width) - 1:
   358→            raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Sized decimal value {value} exceeds width {width}", filename, line, col)
   359→        return value, width
   360→
   361→    raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid literal: {original}", filename, line, col)
   362→
   363→
   364→def parse_3val_input(value_str: str, expected_width: int, signal_name: str, filename: str = None) -> TriValue:
   365→    original = value_str
   366→    text = value_str.replace('_', '')
   367→
   368→    if text in ('0', '1'):
   369→        return TriValue.from_int(int(text), expected_width)
   370→    if text.upper() == 'X':
   371→        return TriValue(0, 0, expected_width)
   372→
   373→    if text.lower().startswith('0b'):
   374→        digits = text[2:]
   375→        if not digits or not re.match(r'^[01Xx]+$', digits):
   376→            raise EvalError("InputValueParseError", f"Invalid input value for '{signal_name}': {original}")
   377→        if len(digits) != expected_width:
   378→            raise EvalError("InputWidthMismatchError",
   379→                           f"Input width mismatch for '{signal_name}': expected width {expected_width}, got width {len(digits)}")
   380→        return _parse_binary_with_x(digits, expected_width)
   381→
   382→    if text.lower().startswith('0x'):
   383→        digits = text[2:]
   384→        if not digits or 'x' in digits.lower() or not re.match(r'^[0-9a-fA-F]+$', digits):
   385→            raise EvalError("InputValueParseError", f"Invalid input value for '{signal_name}': {original}")
   386→        width = len(digits) * 4
   387→        if width != expected_width:

   390→        return TriValue.from_int(int(digits, 16), expected_width)
   391→
   392→    sized_match = re.match(r"^(\d+)'([bhd])([0-9a-fA-FxX_]+)$", text, re.IGNORECASE)
   393→    if sized_match:
   394→        width = int(sized_match.group(1))

   401→            raise EvalError("InputWidthMismatchError",

   403→
   404→        if base == 'b':
   405→            if not re.match(r'^[01Xx]+$', digits):
   406→                raise EvalError("InputValueParseError", f"Invalid input value for '{signal_name}': {original}")
   407→            return _parse_binary_with_x(digits, width)
   408→
   409→        if 'x' in digits.lower():
   410→            raise EvalError("InputValueParseError", f"Invalid input value for '{signal_name}': {original}")
   411→
   412→        if base == 'h':
   413→            if not re.match(r'^[0-9a-fA-F]+$', digits):
   414→                raise EvalError("InputValueParseError", f"Invalid input value for '{signal_name}': {original}")
   415→            value = int(digits, 16)
   416→        else:
   417→            if not re.match(r'^[0-9]+$', digits):
   418→                raise EvalError("InputValueParseError", f"Invalid input value for '{signal_name}': {original}")
   419→            try:
   420→                value = int(digits, 10)
   421→            except ValueError:
   422→                raise EvalError("InputValueParseError", f"Invalid input value for '{signal_name}': {original}")
   423→

   431→def _parse_binary_with_x(digits: str, width: int) -> TriValue:
   432→    value_mask = 0
   433→    known_mask = 0
   434→    for i, digit in enumerate(digits):
   435→        bit_pos = width - 1 - i
   436→        if digit.upper() != 'X':
   437→            known_mask |= (1 << bit_pos)
   438→            if digit == '1':
   439→                value_mask |= (1 << bit_pos)
   440→    return TriValue(value_mask, known_mask, width)

   443→def format_trivalue(value: TriValue) -> str:
   444→    if value.width == 1:
   445→        if not (value.known_mask & 1):
   446→            return 'X'
   447→        return '1' if (value.value_mask & 1) else '0'
   448→    return '0b' + ''.join(value.get_bit(i) for i in range(value.width - 1, -1, -1))
   449→
   450→
   451→def format_value(value: int, width: int, radix: str) -> str:
   452→    if width == 1:
   453→        return str(value & 1)
   454→    if radix == 'bin':
   455→        return f"0b{bin(value)[2:].zfill(width)}"
   456→    if radix == 'hex':
   457→        hex_width = (width + 3) // 4
   458→        return f"0x{hex(value)[2:].zfill(hex_width)}"
   459→    return str(value)
   460→
   461→
   462→def _split_comma_separated(text: str) -> list:
   463→    parts = []
   464→    current = ""
   465→    brace_depth = 0
   466→    paren_depth = 0
   467→    for ch in text:
   468→        if ch == '{':
   469→            brace_depth += 1
   470→            current += ch
   471→        elif ch == '}':
   472→            brace_depth -= 1
   473→            current += ch
   474→        elif ch == '(':
   475→            paren_depth += 1
   476→            current += ch
   477→        elif ch == ')':
   478→            paren_depth -= 1
   479→            current += ch
   480→        elif ch == ',' and brace_depth == 0 and paren_depth == 0:
   481→            parts.append(current.strip())
   482→            current = ""
   483→        else:
   484→            current += ch
   485→    if current.strip():
   486→        parts.append(current.strip())
   487→    return parts
   488→
   489→
   490→class Parser:
   491→    def __init__(self, content: str, filename: str):
   492→        self.filename = filename
   493→        self.lines = content.split('\n')
   494→
   495→    def parse(self) -> 'Circuit':
   496→        circuit = Circuit()
   497→        in_declarations = True
   498→        for line_num, raw_line in enumerate(self.lines, 1):
   499→            stripped = raw_line.strip()
   500→            if not stripped or stripped.startswith('#'):
   501→                continue
   502→            if self._is_declaration(stripped):
   503→                if not in_declarations:
   504→                    raise CircError("DeclarationAfterAssignmentError", "Declaration after assignment is not allowed",
   505→                                   self.filename, line_num, 1)
   506→                self._parse_declaration(stripped, circuit, line_num)
   507→            elif '=' in stripped:
   508→                in_declarations = False
   509→                self._parse_assignment(stripped, circuit, line_num, raw_line)
   510→            else:
   511→                raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid syntax: {stripped}", self.filename, line_num, 1)
   512→        return circuit
   513→
   514→    def _is_declaration(self, line: str) -> bool:
   515→        parts = line.split()
   516→        return '=' not in line and bool(parts) and parts[0].upper() in ('INPUT', 'OUTPUT', 'WIRE')
   517→
   518→    def _parse_declaration(self, line: str, circuit: 'Circuit', line_num: int):
   519→        parts = line.split()
   520→        if not parts:
   521→            return
   522→        keyword = parts[0].upper()
   523→        target_map = {'INPUT': circuit.inputs, 'OUTPUT': circuit.outputs, 'WIRE': circuit.wires}
   524→        if keyword not in target_map:
   525→            return
   526→        target = target_map[keyword]
   527→        existing_names = circuit.get_all_names()
   528→        for part in parts[1:]:
   529→            vec_match = re.match(r'^([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)\[(\d+):(\d+)\]$', part)
   530→            if vec_match:
   531→                name, msb, lsb = vec_match.group(1), int(vec_match.group(2)), int(vec_match.group(3))
   532→                if msb < lsb:
   533→                    raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid vector range: msb ({msb}) must be >= lsb ({lsb})",
   534→                                   self.filename, line_num, line.find(part) + 1)
   535→                if lsb < 0:
   536→                    raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid vector range: lsb ({lsb}) must be >= 0",
   537→                                   self.filename, line_num, line.find(part) + 1)
   538→                if name in existing_names:
   539→                    raise CircError("DuplicateNameError", f"Duplicate name: {name}",
   540→                                   self.filename, line_num, line.find(name) + 1)
   541→                target.append(Signal(name, msb, lsb))
   542→                existing_names.add(name)
   543→            else:
   544→                if not self._is_valid_identifier(part):
   545→                    raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid identifier: {part}",
   546→                                   self.filename, line_num, line.find(part) + 1)
   547→                if part in existing_names:
   548→                    raise CircError("DuplicateNameError", f"Duplicate name: {part}",
   549→                                   self.filename, line_num, line.find(part) + 1)
   550→                target.append(Signal(part, 0, 0))
   551→                existing_names.add(part)
   552→
   553→    @staticmethod
   554→    def _is_valid_identifier(name: str) -> bool:
   555→        return bool(name) and not name[0].isdigit() and all(c.isalnum() or c == '_' for c in name)
   556→
   557→    def _parse_assignment(self, line: str, circuit: 'Circuit', line_num: int, raw_line: str):
   558→        eq_pos = line.find('=')
   559→        lhs = line[:eq_pos].strip()
   560→        rhs = line[eq_pos + 1:].strip()
   561→        if not lhs:
   562→            raise CircError("CircParseError", "Missing left-hand side in assignment", self.filename, line_num, 1)
   563→        if not re.match(r'^([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)$', lhs):
   564→            raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid left-hand side: {lhs}", self.filename, line_num, 1)
   565→        rhs_expr, _ = self._parse_expression(rhs, line_num, eq_pos + 2)
   566→        circuit.assignments.append(Assignment(lhs, rhs_expr, line_num, raw_line.find(lhs) + 1))
   567→
   568→    def _parse_expression(self, text: str, line_num: int, start_col: int) -> Tuple[object, int]:
   569→        text = text.strip()
   570→        if not text:
   571→            raise CircError("CircParseError", "Empty expression", self.filename, line_num, start_col)
   572→        if text.startswith('{'):
   573→            return self._parse_concatenation(text, line_num, start_col)
   574→        expr, consumed = self._parse_primary_expr(text, line_num, start_col)
   575→        remaining = text[consumed:].strip()
   576→        while remaining.startswith('['):
   577→            bracket_end = remaining.find(']')
   578→            if bracket_end == -1:
   579→                raise CircError("CircParseError", "Unmatched bracket in index/slice", self.filename, line_num, start_col + consumed)
   580→            bracket_content = remaining[1:bracket_end]
   581→            if ':' in bracket_content:
   582→                parts = bracket_content.split(':')
   583→                if len(parts) != 2:
   584→                    raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid slice: {bracket_content}", self.filename, line_num, start_col + consumed)
   585→                try:
   586→                    hi, lo = int(parts[0].strip()), int(parts[1].strip())
   587→                except ValueError:
   588→                    raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid slice indices: {bracket_content}", self.filename, line_num, start_col + consumed)
   589→                expr = Slice(expr, hi, lo)
   590→            else:
   591→                try:
   592→                    idx = int(bracket_content.strip())
   593→                except ValueError:
   594→                    raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid index: {bracket_content}", self.filename, line_num, start_col + consumed)
   595→                expr = Index(expr, idx)
   596→            consumed += bracket_end + 1
   597→            remaining = text[consumed:].strip()
   598→        return expr, consumed
   599→
   600→    def _try_match_literal(self, text: str):
   601→        m = re.match(r"^(\d+'[bhd])", text, re.IGNORECASE)
   602→        if m:
   603→            prefix = m.group(1).lower()
   604→            rest = text[len(m.group(1)):]
   605→            if prefix.endswith('b'):
   606→                digits_m = re.match(r'^([01_]+)', rest)
   607→            elif prefix.endswith('h'):
   608→                digits_m = re.match(r'^([0-9a-fA-F_]+)', rest)
   609→            else:
   610→                digits_m = re.match(r'^([0-9_]+)', rest)
   611→            if digits_m:
   612→                full = m.group(1) + digits_m.group(1)
   613→                end_pos = len(full)
   614→                if end_pos >= len(text) or not text[end_pos].isalnum() and text[end_pos] != '_':
   615→                    return full
   616→            return None
   617→        if text.lower().startswith('0b') and len(text) > 2:
   618→            m = re.match(r'^(0b[01_]+)', text, re.IGNORECASE)
   619→            if m:
   620→                end_pos = len(m.group(1))
   621→                if end_pos >= len(text) or not text[end_pos].isalnum() and text[end_pos] != '_':
   622→                    return m.group(1)
   623→            return None
   624→        if text.lower().startswith('0x') and len(text) > 2:
   625→            m = re.match(r'^(0x[0-9a-fA-F_]+)', text, re.IGNORECASE)
   626→            if m:
   627→                end_pos = len(m.group(1))
   628→                if end_pos >= len(text) or not text[end_pos].isalnum() and text[end_pos] != '_':
   629→                    return m.group(1)
   630→            return None
   631→        if text and text[0] in ('0', '1'):
   632→            if len(text) == 1 or not text[1].isalnum() and text[1] != '_':
   633→                return text[0]
   634→        return None
   635→
   636→    def _parse_primary_expr(self, text: str, line_num: int, start_col: int) -> Tuple[object, int]:
   637→        text = text.strip()
   638→        if not text:
   639→            raise CircError("CircParseError", "Empty expression", self.filename, line_num, start_col)
   640→        if text.startswith('{'):
   641→            return self._parse_concatenation(text, line_num, start_col)
   642→        if text.startswith('('):
   643→            depth, i = 1, 1
   644→            while i < len(text) and depth > 0:
   645→                if text[i] == '(':
   646→                    depth += 1
   647→                elif text[i] == ')':
   648→                    depth -= 1
   649→                i += 1
   650→            if depth != 0:
   651→                raise CircError("CircParseError", "Unmatched parenthesis", self.filename, line_num, start_col)
   652→            expr, _ = self._parse_expression(text[1:i-1], line_num, start_col + 1)
   653→            return expr, i
   654→        lit_text = self._try_match_literal(text)
   655→        if lit_text is not None:
   656→            value, width = parse_literal(lit_text, self.filename, line_num, start_col)
   657→            return Literal(value, width), len(lit_text)
   658→        call_match = re.match(r'^([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)\s*\(', text)
   659→        if call_match:
   660→            op_name = call_match.group(1).upper()
   661→            op_start = call_match.end()
   662→            paren_count, i = 1, op_start
   663→            while i < len(text) and paren_count > 0:
   664→                if text[i] == '(':
   665→                    paren_count += 1
   666→                elif text[i] == ')':
   667→                    paren_count -= 1
   668→                i += 1
   669→            if paren_count != 0:
   670→                raise CircError("CircParseError", "Unmatched parenthesis in expression", self.filename, line_num, start_col)
   671→            args = self._parse_arguments(text[op_start:i-1], line_num, start_col + op_start)
   672→            return Call(op_name, args), i
   673→        id_match = re.match(r'^([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)', text)
   674→        if id_match:
   675→            name = id_match.group(1)
   676→            return Identifier(name), len(name)
   677→        raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid expression: {text[:50]}", self.filename, line_num, start_col)
   678→
   679→    def _parse_concatenation(self, text: str, line_num: int, start_col: int) -> Tuple[Concat, int]:
   680→        if not text.startswith('{'):
   681→            raise CircError("CircParseError", "Expected '{' for concatenation", self.filename, line_num, start_col)
   682→        depth, i = 1, 1
   683→        while i < len(text) and depth > 0:
   684→            if text[i] == '{':
   685→                depth += 1
   686→            elif text[i] == '}':
   687→                depth -= 1
   688→            i += 1
   689→        if depth != 0:
   690→            raise CircError("CircParseError", "Unmatched brace in concatenation", self.filename, line_num, start_col)
   691→        inner = text[1:i-1]
   692→        raw_parts = _split_comma_separated(inner)
   693→        if not raw_parts:
   694→            raise CircError("CircParseError", "Empty concatenation", self.filename, line_num, start_col)
   695→        parts = [self._parse_expression(p, line_num, start_col + 1 + idx)[0] for idx, p in enumerate(raw_parts)]
   696→        return Concat(parts), i
   697→
   698→    def _parse_arguments(self, text: str, line_num: int, start_col: int) -> list:
   699→        text = text.strip()
   700→        if not text:
   701→            return []
   702→        raw_parts = _split_comma_separated(text)
   703→        args = []
   704→        offset = 0
   705→        for raw in raw_parts:
   706→            idx = text.index(raw, offset)
   707→            expr, _ = self._parse_expression(raw, line_num, start_col + idx)
   708→            args.append(expr)
   709→            offset = idx + len(raw)
   710→        return args
   711→
   712→
   713→@dataclass
   714→class Assignment:
   715→    lhs: str
   716→    rhs: object
   717→    line: int
   718→    col: int
   719→
   720→
   721→@dataclass
   722→class Circuit:
   723→    inputs: list = field(default_factory=list)
   724→    outputs: list = field(default_factory=list)
   725→    wires: list = field(default_factory=list)
   726→    assignments: list = field(default_factory=list)
   727→
   728→    def get_all_names(self) -> set:
   729→        return {sig.name for sig in self.inputs + self.outputs + self.wires}
   730→
   731→    def get_signal(self, name: str):
   732→        for sig in self.inputs + self.outputs + self.wires:
   733→            if sig.name == name:
   734→                return sig
   735→        return None
   736→
   737→
   738→def parse_json_circuit(content: str, filename: str) -> Circuit:
   739→    """Parse a JSON circuit file."""
   740→    try:
   741→        data = json.loads(content)
   742→    except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
   743→        raise CircError("JsonParseError", f"Invalid JSON: {e.msg}", filename, e.lineno, e.colno)
   744→
   745→    # Validate schema
   746→    if not isinstance(data, dict):
   747→        raise CircError("JsonSchemaError", "JSON root must be an object", filename)
   748→
   749→    if "format_version" not in data:
   750→        raise CircError("JsonSchemaError", "Missing required field: format_version", filename)
   751→
   752→    if data["format_version"] != 1:
   753→        raise CircError("JsonSchemaError", f"Unsupported format_version: {data.get('format_version')}, expected 1", filename)
   754→
   755→    for field_name in ("inputs", "outputs", "wires", "assignments"):
   756→        if field_name not in data:
   757→            raise CircError("JsonSchemaError", f"Missing required field: {field_name}", filename)
   758→        if not isinstance(data[field_name], list):
   759→            raise CircError("JsonSchemaError", f"Field '{field_name}' must be an array", filename)
   760→
   761→    circuit = Circuit()
   762→    all_names = set()
   763→
   764→    # Parse ports
   765→    def parse_port(port_data, target_list, kind):
   766→        if not isinstance(port_data, dict):
   767→            raise CircError("JsonSchemaError", f"{kind} must be an object", filename)
   768→        if "name" not in port_data:
   769→            raise CircError("JsonSchemaError", f"{kind} missing required field: name", filename)
   770→        name = port_data["name"]
   771→        if not isinstance(name, str):
   772→            raise CircError("JsonSchemaError", f"{kind} name must be a string", filename)
   773→        if not re.match(r'^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*$', name):
   774→            raise CircError("JsonSchemaError", f"Invalid identifier: {name}", filename)
   775→
   776→        msb = port_data.get("msb", 0)
   777→        lsb = port_data.get("lsb", 0)
   778→        if not isinstance(msb, int) or not isinstance(lsb, int):
   779→            raise CircError("JsonSchemaError", f"{kind} msb and lsb must be integers", filename)
   780→        if msb < lsb:
   781→            raise CircError("JsonSchemaError", f"{kind} msb ({msb}) must be >= lsb ({lsb})", filename)
   782→        if lsb < 0:
   783→            raise CircError("JsonSchemaError", f"{kind} lsb ({lsb}) must be >= 0", filename)
   784→
   785→        if name in all_names:
   786→            raise CircError("DuplicateNameError", f"Duplicate name: {name}", filename)
   787→        all_names.add(name)
   788→        target_list.append(Signal(name, msb, lsb))
   789→
   790→    for port in data["inputs"]:
   791→        parse_port(port, circuit.inputs, "Input port")
   792→    for port in data["outputs"]:
   793→        parse_port(port, circuit.outputs, "Output port")
   794→    for port in data["wires"]:
   795→        parse_port(port, circuit.wires, "Wire port")
   796→
   797→    # Parse assignments
   798→    assigned_lhs = set()
   799→    for i, asn_data in enumerate(data["assignments"]):
   800→        if not isinstance(asn_data, dict):
   801→            raise CircError("JsonSchemaError", f"Assignment {i} must be an object", filename)
   802→        if "lhs" not in asn_data:
   803→            raise CircError("JsonSchemaError", f"Assignment {i} missing required field: lhs", filename)
   804→        if "rhs" not in asn_data:
   805→            raise CircError("JsonSchemaError", f"Assignment {i} missing required field: rhs", filename)
   806→
   807→        lhs = asn_data["lhs"]
   808→        rhs_str = asn_data["rhs"]
   809→
   810→        if not isinstance(lhs, str):
   811→            raise CircError("JsonSchemaError", f"Assignment {i} lhs must be a string", filename)
   812→        if not isinstance(rhs_str, str):
   813→            raise CircError("JsonSchemaError", f"Assignment {i} rhs must be a string", filename)
   814→
   815→        # Check that lhs is declared
   816→        if lhs not in all_names:
   817→            raise CircError("UndefinedNameError", f"Undefined signal: {lhs}", filename)
   818→
   819→        # Check that every output and wire appears exactly once as LHS
   820→        if lhs in assigned_lhs:
   821→            raise CircError("RedefinitionError", f"Signal assigned multiple times: {lhs}", filename)
   822→        assigned_lhs.add(lhs)
   823→
   824→        # Parse RHS expression using existing parser
   825→        parser = Parser("", filename)
   826→        try:
   827→            rhs_expr, _ = parser._parse_expression(rhs_str, 1, 1)
   828→        except CircError as e:
   829→            raise CircError(e.error_type, e.message, filename, 1, e.col)
   830→
   831→        circuit.assignments.append(Assignment(lhs, rhs_expr, 1, 1))
   832→
   833→    # Check that all outputs and wires are assigned
   834→    for sig in circuit.outputs + circuit.wires:
   835→        if sig.name not in assigned_lhs:
   836→            kind = "Output" if sig in circuit.outputs else "Wire"
   837→            raise CircError("UnassignedSignalError", f"{kind} not assigned: {sig.name}", filename)
   838→
   839→    return circuit
   840→

   842→def parse_bench_circuit(content: str, filename: str) -> Circuit:
   843→    """Parse a BENCH circuit file."""
   844→    circuit = Circuit()
   845→    all_names = set()
   846→    defined_wires = set()  # Wires defined by assignment
   847→    output_refs = set()  # OUTPUT() references
   848→    assignments_list = []  # [(lhs, rhs_str, line_num)]
   849→
   850→    lines = content.split('\n')
   851→
   852→    for line_num, line in enumerate(lines, 1):
   853→        # Remove comments
   854→        if '#' in line:
   855→            line = line[:line.index('#')]
   856→        line = line.strip()
   857→        if not line:
   858→            continue
   859→
   860→        # Check for brackets in the line (not allowed in BENCH)
   861→        if '[' in line or ']' in line:
   862→            raise CircError("BenchParseError", "BENCH format does not support vector signals (brackets not allowed)", filename, line_num)
   863→
   864→        # Parse INPUT(name)
   865→        input_match = re.match(r'^INPUT\s*\(\s*([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)\s*\)$', line, re.IGNORECASE)
   866→        if input_match:
   867→            name = input_match.group(1)
   868→            if name in all_names:
   869→                raise CircError("RedefinitionError", f"Signal defined multiple times: {name}", filename, line_num)
   870→            all_names.add(name)
   871→            circuit.inputs.append(Signal(name, 0, 0))
   872→            continue
   873→
   874→        # Parse OUTPUT(name)
   875→        output_match = re.match(r'^OUTPUT\s*\(\s*([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)\s*\)$', line, re.IGNORECASE)
   876→        if output_match:
   877→            name = output_match.group(1)
   878→            if name in output_refs:
   879→                raise CircError("RedefinitionError", f"OUTPUT declared multiple times: {name}", filename, line_num)
   880→            output_refs.add(name)
   881→            circuit.outputs.append(Signal(name, 0, 0))
   882→            continue
   883→
   884→        # Parse assignment: lhs = OP(args)
   885→        assign_match = re.match(r'^([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)\s*=\s*(.+)$', line)
   886→        if assign_match:
   887→            lhs = assign_match.group(1)
   888→            rhs_str = assign_match.group(2).strip()
   889→
   890→            if lhs in defined_wires:
   891→                raise CircError("RedefinitionError", f"Signal assigned multiple times: {lhs}", filename, line_num)
   892→
   893→            # Check if lhs is an input (cannot assign to inputs)
   894→            if lhs in all_names and lhs not in output_refs:
   895→                raise CircError("InputAssignmentError", f"Cannot assign to input: {lhs}", filename, line_num)
   896→
   897→            # Add to defined wires and all_names
   898→            defined_wires.add(lhs)
   899→            all_names.add(lhs)
   900→
   901→            # Store assignment for later processing
   902→            assignments_list.append((lhs, rhs_str, line_num))
   903→            continue
   904→
   905→        raise CircError("BenchParseError", f"Invalid syntax: {line}", filename, line_num)
   906→
   907→    # Add wire declarations for defined wires (excluding outputs that reference inputs or other wires)
   908→    wire_names = defined_wires - output_refs
   909→    for name in wire_names:
   910→        circuit.wires.append(Signal(name, 0, 0))
   911→
   912→    # Now parse all assignments
   913→    parser = Parser("", filename)
   914→    for lhs, rhs_str, line_num in assignments_list:
   915→        # Parse RHS - it must be a function call in BENCH format
   916→        try:
   917→            rhs_expr, _ = parser._parse_expression(rhs_str, line_num, 1)
   918→        except CircError as e:
   919→            raise CircError(e.error_type, e.message, filename, line_num, e.col)
   920→
   921→        # Validate BENCH-specific constraints
   922→        _validate_bench_expr(rhs_expr, filename, line_num, all_names)
   923→
   924→        circuit.assignments.append(Assignment(lhs, rhs_expr, line_num, 1))
   925→
   926→    # Validate OUTPUT references - must reference either an input or a defined wire
   927→    input_names = {sig.name for sig in circuit.inputs}
   928→    for output_sig in circuit.outputs:
   929→        name = output_sig.name
   930→        if name not in all_names:
   931→            raise CircError("UndefinedNameError", f"OUTPUT references undefined signal: {name}", filename)
   932→        if name not in defined_wires and name not in input_names:
   933→            raise CircError("UndefinedNameError", f"OUTPUT references undefined signal: {name}", filename)
   934→
   935→    return circuit
   936→
   937→
   938→def _validate_bench_expr(expr, filename: str, line_num: int, defined_names: set):
   939→    """Validate that a BENCH expression contains no literals and uses defined names."""
   940→    if isinstance(expr, Literal):
   941→        raise CircError("BenchParseError", "Literals are not allowed in BENCH format", filename, line_num)
   942→
   943→    if isinstance(expr, Identifier):
   944→        if expr.name not in defined_names:
   945→            raise CircError("UndefinedNameError", f"Undefined signal: {expr.name}", filename, line_num)
   946→        return
   947→
   948→    if isinstance(expr, Call):
   949→        for arg in expr.args:
   950→            _validate_bench_expr(arg, filename, line_num, defined_names)
   951→        return
   952→
   953→    if isinstance(expr, Index):
   954→        raise CircError("BenchParseError", "Indexing is not allowed in BENCH format", filename, line_num)
   955→
   956→    if isinstance(expr, Slice):
   957→        raise CircError("BenchParseError", "Slicing is not allowed in BENCH format", filename, line_num)
   958→
   959→    if isinstance(expr, Concat):
   960→        raise CircError("BenchParseError", "Concatenation is not allowed in BENCH format", filename, line_num)
   961→

   963→class Validator:
   964→    def __init__(self, circuit: Circuit, filename: str):
   965→        self.circuit = circuit
   966→        self.filename = filename
   967→        self.signal_types = {sig.name: 'input' for sig in circuit.inputs}
   968→        self.signal_types.update({sig.name: 'output' for sig in circuit.outputs})
   969→        self.signal_types.update({sig.name: 'wire' for sig in circuit.wires})
   970→        self.signal_info = {sig.name: sig for sig in circuit.inputs + circuit.outputs + circuit.wires}
   971→        self._all_names = circuit.get_all_names()
   972→        self._signal_widths = {name: sig.width for name, sig in self.signal_info.items()}
   973→        # Track signals that are both inputs and outputs (BENCH pass-through case)
   974→        self._input_names = {sig.name for sig in circuit.inputs}
   975→
   976→    def validate(self):
   977→        self._check_assignments()
   978→        self._check_cycles()
   979→
   980→    def _check_assignments(self):
   981→        assigned = set()
   982→        for asn in self.circuit.assignments:
   983→            if asn.lhs not in self._all_names:
   984→                raise CircError("UndefinedNameError", f"Undefined signal: {asn.lhs}", self.filename, asn.line, asn.col)
   985→            if self.signal_types.get(asn.lhs) == 'input':
   986→                raise CircError("InputAssignmentError", f"Cannot assign to input: {asn.lhs}", self.filename, asn.line, asn.col)
   987→            if asn.lhs in assigned:
   988→                raise CircError("MultipleAssignmentError", f"Signal assigned multiple times: {asn.lhs}", self.filename, asn.line, asn.col)
   989→            assigned.add(asn.lhs)
   990→            self._validate_expr(asn.rhs, asn.line)
   991→        for sig in self.circuit.outputs + self.circuit.wires:
   992→            if sig.name not in assigned:
   993→                # Skip if this output is also an input (BENCH pass-through)
   994→                if sig.name in self._input_names and self.signal_types.get(sig.name) == 'output':
   995→                    continue
   996→                kind = "Output" if self.signal_types.get(sig.name) == 'output' else "Wire"
   997→                raise CircError("UnassignedSignalError", f"{kind} not assigned: {sig.name}", self.filename)
   998→
   999→    def _validate_expr(self, expr, line_num: int) -> int:
  1000→        if isinstance(expr, Literal):
  1001→            return expr.width
  1002→        if isinstance(expr, Identifier):
  1003→            if expr.name not in self._all_names:
  1004→                raise CircError("UndefinedNameError", f"Undefined signal: {expr.name}", self.filename, line_num)
  1005→            return self._signal_widths[expr.name]
  1006→        if isinstance(expr, Index):
  1007→            self._validate_expr(expr.expr, line_num)
  1008→            self._check_index_bounds(expr, line_num)
  1009→            return 1
  1010→        if isinstance(expr, Slice):
  1011→            self._validate_expr(expr.expr, line_num)
  1012→            if expr.hi < expr.lo:
  1013→                raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid slice: hi ({expr.hi}) < lo ({expr.lo})",
  1014→                               self.filename, line_num)
  1015→            self._check_slice_bounds(expr, line_num)
  1016→            return expr.hi - expr.lo + 1
  1017→        if isinstance(expr, Concat):
  1018→            return sum(self._validate_expr(p, line_num) for p in expr.parts)
  1019→        if isinstance(expr, Call):
  1020→            self._check_operator_arity(expr, line_num)
  1021→            return self._check_operator_width(expr, line_num)
  1022→        raise RuntimeError(f"Unknown expression type: {type(expr)}")
  1023→
  1024→    def _check_index_bounds(self, expr: Index, line_num: int):
  1025→        sig = self.signal_info.get(expr.expr.name) if isinstance(expr.expr, Identifier) else None
  1026→        if sig:
  1027→            if expr.index < sig.lsb or expr.index > sig.msb:
  1028→                raise CircError("IndexOutOfBoundsError",
  1029→                               f"Index {expr.index} out of bounds for signal {sig.name}[{sig.msb}:{sig.lsb}]",
  1030→                               self.filename, line_num)
  1031→        else:
  1032→            base_width = self._validate_expr(expr.expr, line_num)
  1033→            if expr.index < 0 or expr.index >= base_width:
  1034→                raise CircError("IndexOutOfBoundsError",
  1035→                               f"Index {expr.index} out of bounds for expression of width {base_width}",
  1036→                               self.filename, line_num)
  1037→
  1038→    def _check_slice_bounds(self, expr: Slice, line_num: int):
  1039→        sig = self.signal_info.get(expr.expr.name) if isinstance(expr.expr, Identifier) else None
  1040→        if sig:
  1041→            if expr.lo < sig.lsb or expr.hi > sig.msb:
  1042→                raise CircError("IndexOutOfBoundsError",
  1043→                               f"Slice [{expr.hi}:{expr.lo}] out of bounds for signal {sig.name}[{sig.msb}:{sig.lsb}]",
  1044→                               self.filename, line_num)
  1045→        else:
  1046→            base_width = self._validate_expr(expr.expr, line_num)
  1047→            if expr.lo < 0 or expr.hi >= base_width:
  1048→                raise CircError("IndexOutOfBoundsError",
  1049→                               f"Slice [{expr.hi}:{expr.lo}] out of bounds for expression of width {base_width}",
  1050→                               self.filename, line_num)
  1051→
  1052→    def _check_operator_arity(self, call: Call, line_num: int):
  1053→        op = call.operator
  1054→        num_args = len(call.args)
  1055→        if op not in OPERATOR_ARITY:
  1056→            raise CircError("UndefinedNameError", f"Unknown operator: {op}", self.filename, line_num)
  1057→        min_args, max_args = OPERATOR_ARITY[op]
  1058→        if num_args < min_args or (max_args and num_args > max_args):
  1059→            if max_args == min_args:
  1060→                req = f"exactly {min_args}"
  1061→            elif max_args is None:
  1062→                req = f"at least {min_args}"
  1063→            else:
  1064→                req = f"between {min_args} and {max_args}"
  1065→            plural = "s" if min_args > 1 or (max_args and max_args > 1) else ""
  1066→            raise CircError("ArityError", f"Operator {op} requires {req} argument{plural}, got {num_args}", self.filename, line_num)
  1067→
  1068→    def _check_operator_width(self, call: Call, line_num: int) -> int:
  1069→        op = call.operator
  1070→        arg_widths = [self._validate_expr(arg, line_num) for arg in call.args]
  1071→        if op in _UNARY_PROPAGATE:
  1072→            return arg_widths[0]
  1073→        if op in _BINARY_PROPAGATE:
  1074→            for i, w in enumerate(arg_widths[1:], 1):
  1075→                if w != arg_widths[0]:
  1076→                    raise CircError("WidthMismatchError",
  1077→                                   f"Width mismatch in {op}: operand 0 has width {arg_widths[0]}, operand {i} has width {w}",
  1078→                                   self.filename, line_num)
  1079→            return arg_widths[0]
  1080→        if op in _SELECT:
  1081→            if arg_widths[0] != 1:
  1082→                raise CircError("WidthMismatchError",
  1083→                               f"Selector in {op} must have width 1, got width {arg_widths[0]}", self.filename, line_num)
  1084→            if arg_widths[1] != arg_widths[2]:
  1085→                raise CircError("WidthMismatchError",
  1086→                               f"Width mismatch in {op}: operand 1 has width {arg_widths[1]}, operand 2 has width {arg_widths[2]}",
  1087→                               self.filename, line_num)
  1088→            return arg_widths[1]
  1089→        if op in _REDUCE:
  1090→            return 1
  1091→        if op == 'EQ':
  1092→            if arg_widths[0] != arg_widths[1]:
  1093→                raise CircError("WidthMismatchError",
  1094→                               f"Width mismatch in EQ: operand 0 has width {arg_widths[0]}, operand 1 has width {arg_widths[1]}",
  1095→                               self.filename, line_num)
  1096→            return 1
  1097→        raise RuntimeError(f"Unknown operator: {op}")
  1098→
  1099→    def _check_cycles(self):
  1100→        dependencies = {sig.name: set() for sig in self.circuit.inputs}
  1101→        for asn in self.circuit.assignments:
  1102→            dependencies[asn.lhs] = _collect_dependencies(asn.rhs)
  1103→        WHITE, GRAY, BLACK = 0, 1, 2
  1104→        color = {name: WHITE for name in self._all_names}
  1105→
  1106→        def dfs(node: str, path: list):
  1107→            color[node] = GRAY
  1108→            path.append(node)
  1109→            for dep in dependencies.get(node, set()):
  1110→                if color[dep] == GRAY:
  1111→                    cycle_start = path.index(dep)
  1112→                    return path[cycle_start:] + [dep]
  1113→                if color[dep] == WHITE:
  1114→                    result = dfs(dep, path)
  1115→                    if result:
  1116→                        return result
  1117→            path.pop()
  1118→            color[node] = BLACK
  1119→            return None
  1120→
  1121→        for node in self._all_names:
  1122→            if color[node] == WHITE:
  1123→                cycle = dfs(node, [])
  1124→                if cycle:
  1125→                    raise CircError("CycleError", f"Cycle detected: {' -> '.join(cycle)}", self.filename)
  1126→

  1128→class Evaluator:
  1129→    """Evaluates a circuit using 3-valued logic. For 2-valued mode, inputs are
  1130→    fully-known TriValues and results are converted to int at output time."""
  1131→
  1132→    def __init__(self, circuit: Circuit):
  1133→        self.circuit = circuit
  1134→        self._signal_widths = _all_signal_widths(circuit)
  1135→
  1136→    def evaluate(self, inputs: dict) -> dict:
  1137→        self.values = dict(inputs)
  1138→        for asn in self.circuit.assignments:
  1139→            self.values[asn.lhs] = self._eval_expr(asn.rhs)
  1140→        return {sig.name: self.values[sig.name] for sig in self.circuit.outputs}
  1141→
  1142→    def _eval_expr(self, expr) -> TriValue:
  1143→        if isinstance(expr, Literal):
  1144→            return TriValue.from_int(expr.value, expr.width)
  1145→        if isinstance(expr, Identifier):
  1146→            return self.values[expr.name]
  1147→        if isinstance(expr, Index):
  1148→            val = self._eval_expr(expr.expr)
  1149→            return TriValue(
  1150→                (val.value_mask >> expr.index) & 1,
  1151→                (val.known_mask >> expr.index) & 1,
  1152→                1
  1153→            )
  1154→        if isinstance(expr, Slice):
  1155→            val = self._eval_expr(expr.expr)
  1156→            width = expr.hi - expr.lo + 1
  1157→            mask = (1 << width) - 1
  1158→            return TriValue(
  1159→                (val.value_mask >> expr.lo) & mask,
  1160→                (val.known_mask >> expr.lo) & mask,
  1161→                width
  1162→            )
  1163→        if isinstance(expr, Concat):
  1164→            result_value = 0
  1165→            result_known = 0
  1166→            total_width = 0
  1167→            for part in expr.parts:
  1168→                w = _expr_width(part, self._signal_widths)
  1169→                part_val = self._eval_expr(part)
  1170→                result_value = (result_value << w) | (part_val.value_mask & ((1 << w) - 1))
  1171→                result_known = (result_known << w) | (part_val.known_mask & ((1 << w) - 1))
  1172→                total_width += w
  1173→            return TriValue(result_value, result_known, total_width)
  1174→        if isinstance(expr, Call):
  1175→            args = [self._eval_expr(arg) for arg in expr.args]
  1176→            return self._apply_operator(expr.operator, args)
  1177→        raise RuntimeError(f"Unknown expression type: {type(expr)}")
  1178→
  1179→    def _apply_operator(self, op: str, args: list) -> TriValue:
  1180→        if op == 'NOT':
  1181→            return TriValue.not_(args[0])
  1182→        if op == 'BUF':
  1183→            return args[0].copy()
  1184→
  1185→        if op in _BINARY_OPS:
  1186→            result = args[0]
  1187→            for a in args[1:]:
  1188→                result = _BINARY_OPS[op](result, a)
  1189→            if op in _NEGATED:
  1190→                result = TriValue.not_(result)
  1191→            return result
  1192→
  1193→        if op in _SELECT:
  1194→            return TriValue.mux(args[0], args[1], args[2])
  1195→
  1196→        if op == 'REDUCE_AND':
  1197→            return TriValue.reduce_and(args[0])
  1198→        if op == 'REDUCE_OR':
  1199→            return TriValue.reduce_or(args[0])
  1200→        if op == 'REDUCE_XOR':
  1201→            return TriValue.reduce_xor(args[0])
  1202→        if op == 'EQ':
  1203→            return TriValue.eq(args[0], args[1])
  1204→
  1205→        raise RuntimeError(f"Unknown operator: {op}")
  1206→

  1212→def detect_format(filename: str) -> str:
  1213→    """Detect format from file extension."""
  1214→    if filename.endswith('.circ'):
  1215→        return 'circ'
  1216→    if filename.endswith('.json'):
  1217→        return 'json'
  1218→    if filename.endswith('.bench'):
  1219→        return 'bench'
  1220→    return None

  1223→def load_circuit(filename: str, command: str, json_mode: bool, format_override: str = None):
  1224→    try:
  1225→        with open(filename, 'r') as f:
  1226→            content = f.read()
  1227→    except (FileNotFoundError, IOError):
  1228→        return output_error(CircError("FileNotFoundError", f"Cannot read file: {filename}", filename), command, json_mode)
  1229→
  1230→    # Determine format
  1231→    if format_override and format_override != 'auto':
  1232→        format_name = format_override
  1233→    else:
  1234→        format_name = detect_format(filename)
  1235→        if format_name is None:
  1236→            return output_error(CircError("UnknownInputFormatError", f"Cannot determine format from extension: {filename}"), command, json_mode)
  1237→
  1238→    try:
  1239→        if format_name == 'circ':
  1240→            circuit = Parser(content, filename).parse()
  1241→            Validator(circuit, filename).validate()
  1242→        elif format_name == 'json':
  1243→            circuit = parse_json_circuit(content, filename)
  1244→            Validator(circuit, filename).validate()
  1245→        elif format_name == 'bench':
  1246→            circuit = parse_bench_circuit(content, filename)
  1247→            Validator(circuit, filename).validate()
  1248→        else:
  1249→            return output_error(CircError("UnknownInputFormatError", f"Unknown format: {format_name}"), command, json_mode)
  1250→    except CircError as e:
  1251→        return output_error(e, command, json_mode)
  1252→
  1253→    return circuit, format_name

  1256→def signal_list(signals):

  1260→def get_exit_code(error_type: str) -> int:
  1261→    if error_type in VALIDATION_ERRORS:
  1262→        return EXIT_VALIDATION_ERROR
  1263→    return ERROR_EXIT_CODES.get(error_type, 1)

  1266→def output_error(error, command: str, json_mode: bool) -> int:
  1267→    exit_code = get_exit_code(error.error_type)
  1268→    if json_mode:
  1269→        output_json({"ok": False, "command": command, "exit_code": exit_code, "error": error.to_dict()})
  1270→    else:
  1271→        loc = ""
  1272→        if hasattr(error, 'file') and error.file:
  1273→            loc = f"{error.file}:"
  1274→            if error.line:
  1275→                loc += f"{error.line}:"
  1276→                if error.col:
  1277→                    loc += f"{error.col}:"
  1278→            loc += " "
  1279→        print(f"{loc}Error: {error.message}", file=sys.stderr)
  1280→    return exit_code

  1283→def check_command(args: list, json_mode: bool, format_override: str = None) -> int:
  1284→    if not args:
  1285→        return output_error(CircError("CliUsageError", "Missing required argument: <file.circ>"), "__cli__", json_mode)
  1286→    filename = args[0]
  1287→    result = load_circuit(filename, "check", json_mode, format_override)
  1288→    if isinstance(result, int):
  1289→        return result
  1290→    circuit, format_name = result
  1291→    inputs = signal_list(circuit.inputs)
  1292→    outputs = signal_list(circuit.outputs)
  1293→    if json_mode:
  1294→        output_json({"ok": True, "command": "check", "format": format_name, "inputs": inputs, "outputs": outputs})
  1295→    else:
  1296→        print("Circuit is valid.")
  1297→        print(f"Inputs: {', '.join(s['name'] for s in inputs)}")
  1298→        print(f"Outputs: {', '.join(s['name'] for s in outputs)}")
  1299→    return 0

  1302→def _parse_eval_args(args, json_mode):
  1303→    """Parse eval command arguments. Returns (opts_dict, error_result_or_None)."""
  1304→    if not args:
  1305→        return None, output_error(CircError("CliUsageError", "Missing required argument: <file.circ>"), "__cli__", json_mode)
  1306→    opts = {
  1307→        'filename': args[0],
  1308→        'set_values': {},
  1309→        'default_value': None,
  1310→        'allow_extra': False,
  1311→        'radix': 'bin',
  1312→        'mode': '2val',
  1313→        'format': 'auto',
  1314→    }
  1315→    i = 1
  1316→    while i < len(args):
  1317→        arg = args[i]
  1318→        if arg == '--set':
  1319→            if i + 1 >= len(args):
  1320→                return None, output_error(CircError("CliUsageError", "--set requires a name=value argument"), "eval", json_mode)
  1321→            set_arg = args[i + 1]
  1322→            if '=' not in set_arg:
  1323→                return None, output_error(CircError("CliUsageError", f"Invalid --set format: {set_arg}, expected name=value"), "eval", json_mode)
  1324→            name, value = set_arg.split('=', 1)
  1325→            opts['set_values'][name] = value
  1326→            i += 2
  1327→        elif arg == '--default':
  1328→            if i + 1 >= len(args):
  1329→                return None, output_error(CircError("CliUsageError", "--default requires a value (0 or 1)"), "eval", json_mode)
  1330→            default_arg = args[i + 1]
  1331→            if default_arg not in ('0', '1'):
  1332→                return None, output_error(CircError("CliUsageError", f"Invalid --default value: {default_arg}, must be 0 or 1"), "eval", json_mode)
  1333→            opts['default_value'] = int(default_arg)
  1334→            i += 2
  1335→        elif arg == '--allow-extra':
  1336→            opts['allow_extra'] = True
  1337→            i += 1
  1338→        elif arg == '--radix':
  1339→            if i + 1 >= len(args):
  1340→                return None, output_error(CircError("CliUsageError", "--radix requires a value (bin, hex, or dec)"), "eval", json_mode)
  1341→            radix_arg = args[i + 1].lower()
  1342→            if radix_arg not in ('bin', 'hex', 'dec'):
  1343→                return None, output_error(CircError("CliUsageError", f"Invalid --radix value: {radix_arg}, must be bin, hex, or dec"), "eval", json_mode)
  1344→            opts['radix'] = radix_arg
  1345→            i += 2
  1346→        elif arg == '--mode':
  1347→            if i + 1 >= len(args):
  1348→                return None, output_error(CircError("CliUsageError", "--mode requires a value (2val or 3val)"), "eval", json_mode)
  1349→            mode_arg = args[i + 1].lower()
  1350→            if mode_arg not in ('2val', '3val'):
  1351→                return None, output_error(CircError("CliUsageError", f"Invalid --mode value: {mode_arg}, must be 2val or 3val"), "eval", json_mode)
  1352→            opts['mode'] = mode_arg
  1353→            i += 2
  1354→        elif arg == '--format':
  1355→            if i + 1 >= len(args):
  1356→                return None, output_error(CircError("CliUsageError", "--format requires a value (auto, circ, json, or bench)"), "eval", json_mode)
  1357→            format_arg = args[i + 1].lower()
  1358→            if format_arg not in ('auto', 'circ', 'json', 'bench'):
  1359→                return None, output_error(CircError("CliUsageError", f"Invalid --format value: {format_arg}, must be auto, circ, json, or bench"), "eval", json_mode)
  1360→            opts['format'] = format_arg
  1361→            i += 2
  1362→        elif '=' in arg:
  1363→            name, value = arg.split('=', 1)
  1364→            opts['set_values'][name] = value
  1365→            i += 1
  1366→        else:
  1367→            return None, output_error(CircError("CliUsageError", f"Unknown option: {arg}"), "eval", json_mode)
  1368→    return opts, None

  1371→def _default_fill(default_value, width):
  1372→    """Compute the fill value for a signal when using --default."""
  1373→    if width == 1:

    57→class ExprVisitor(Protocol):

    59→    def visit_identifier(self, expr: Identifier): ...

    66→def accept(expr, visitor: ExprVisitor):
    67→    if isinstance(expr, Literal):
    68→        return visitor.visit_literal(expr)
    69→    if isinstance(expr, Identifier):
    70→        return visitor.visit_identifier(expr)
    71→    if isinstance(expr, Index):
    72→        return visitor.visit_index(expr)
    73→    if isinstance(expr, Slice):
    74→        return visitor.visit_slice(expr)
    75→    if isinstance(expr, Concat):
    76→        return visitor.visit_concat(expr)
    77→    if isinstance(expr, Call):

    79→    raise TypeError(f"Unknown expression type: {type(expr)}")

    82→def collect_dependencies(expr) -> set:

    94→        for arg in expr.args:

   113→def _parse_binary_with_x(digits: str, width: int):
   114→    """Parse binary digits containing X. Returns (value_mask, known_mask) tuple."""
   115→    from trivalue import TriValue
   116→    value_mask = 0
   117→    known_mask = 0
   118→    for i, digit in enumerate(digits):
   119→        bit_pos = width - 1 - i
   120→        if digit.upper() != 'X':
   121→            known_mask |= (1 << bit_pos)
   122→            if digit == '1':
   123→                value_mask |= (1 << bit_pos)
   124→    return TriValue(value_mask, known_mask, width)
   125→
   126→
   127→def parse_3val_input(value_str: str, expected_width: int, signal_name: str, filename: str = None):
   128→    from trivalue import TriValue
   129→    from errors import EvalError
   130→
   131→    original = value_str
   132→    text = value_str.replace('_', '')
   133→
   134→    if text in ('0', '1'):
   135→        return TriValue.from_int(int(text), expected_width)
   136→    if text.upper() == 'X':
   137→        return TriValue(0, 0, expected_width)
   138→
   139→    def _width_err(expected, got):
   140→        return EvalError("InputWidthMismatchError",
   141→                         f"Input width mismatch for '{signal_name}': expected width {expected}, got width {got}")
   142→    def _val_err():
   143→        return EvalError("InputValueParseError", f"Invalid input value for '{signal_name}': {original}")
   144→
   145→    if text.lower().startswith('0b'):
   146→        digits = text[2:]
   147→        if not digits or not re.match(r'^[01Xx]+$', digits):
   148→            raise _val_err()
   149→        if len(digits) != expected_width:
   150→            raise _width_err(expected_width, len(digits))
   151→        return _parse_binary_with_x(digits, expected_width)
   152→
   153→    if text.lower().startswith('0x'):
   154→        digits = text[2:]
   155→        if not digits or 'x' in digits.lower() or not re.match(r'^[0-9a-fA-F]+$', digits):
   156→            raise _val_err()
   157→        width = len(digits) * 4
   158→        if width != expected_width:
   159→            raise _width_err(expected_width, width)
   160→        return TriValue.from_int(int(digits, 16), expected_width)
   161→
   162→    sized_match = re.match(r"^(\d+)'([bhd])([0-9a-fA-FxX_]+)$", text, re.IGNORECASE)
   163→    if sized_match:
   164→        width = int(sized_match.group(1))
   165→        base = sized_match.group(2).lower()
   166→        digits = sized_match.group(3).replace('_', '')
   167→
   168→        if width <= 0:
   169→            raise _val_err()
   170→        if width != expected_width:
   171→            raise _width_err(expected_width, width)
   172→
   173→        if base == 'b':
   174→            if not re.match(r'^[01Xx]+$', digits):
   175→                raise _val_err()
   176→            return _parse_binary_with_x(digits, width)
   177→
   178→        if 'x' in digits.lower():
   179→            raise _val_err()
   180→
   181→        if base == 'h':
   182→            if not re.match(r'^[0-9a-fA-F]+$', digits):
   183→                raise _val_err()
   184→            value = int(digits, 16)
   185→        else:
   186→            if not re.match(r'^[0-9]+$', digits):
   187→                raise _val_err()
   188→            try:
   189→                value = int(digits, 10)
   190→            except ValueError:
   191→                raise _val_err()
   192→
   193→        if value > (1 << width) - 1:
   194→            raise _val_err()
   195→        return TriValue.from_int(value, width)
   196→
   197→    raise _val_err()
   198→

   200→def _is_valid_identifier(name: str) -> bool:
   201→    return bool(name) and not name[0].isdigit() and all(c.isalnum() or c == '_' for c in name)

   204→class CircParser:
   205→    def __init__(self, content: str, filename: str):
   206→        self.filename = filename
   207→        self.lines = content.split('\n')
   208→
   209→    def parse(self) -> Circuit:
   210→        circuit = Circuit()
   211→        in_declarations = True
   212→        for line_num, raw_line in enumerate(self.lines, 1):
   213→            stripped = raw_line.strip()
   214→            if not stripped or stripped.startswith('#'):
   215→                continue
   216→            if self._is_declaration(stripped):
   217→                if not in_declarations:
   218→                    raise CircError("DeclarationAfterAssignmentError", "Declaration after assignment is not allowed",
   219→                                   self.filename, line_num, 1)
   220→                self._parse_declaration(stripped, circuit, line_num)
   221→            elif '=' in stripped:
   222→                in_declarations = False
   223→                self._parse_assignment(stripped, circuit, line_num, raw_line)
   224→            else:
   225→                raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid syntax: {stripped}", self.filename, line_num, 1)
   226→        return circuit
   227→
   228→    def _is_declaration(self, line: str) -> bool:
   229→        parts = line.split()
   230→        return '=' not in line and bool(parts) and parts[0].upper() in ('INPUT', 'OUTPUT', 'WIRE')
   231→
   232→    def _parse_declaration(self, line: str, circuit: Circuit, line_num: int):
   233→        parts = line.split()
   234→        if not parts:
   235→            return
   236→        keyword = parts[0].upper()
   237→        target_map = {'INPUT': circuit.inputs, 'OUTPUT': circuit.outputs, 'WIRE': circuit.wires}
   238→        if keyword not in target_map:
   239→            return
   240→        target = target_map[keyword]
   241→        existing_names = circuit.get_all_names()
   242→        for part in parts[1:]:
   243→            vec_match = re.match(r'^([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)\[(\d+):(\d+)\]$', part)
   244→            if vec_match:
   245→                name, msb, lsb = vec_match.group(1), int(vec_match.group(2)), int(vec_match.group(3))
   246→                if msb < lsb:
   247→                    raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid vector range: msb ({msb}) must be >= lsb ({lsb})",
   248→                                   self.filename, line_num, line.find(part) + 1)
   249→                if lsb < 0:
   250→                    raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid vector range: lsb ({lsb}) must be >= 0",
   251→                                   self.filename, line_num, line.find(part) + 1)
   252→                if name in existing_names:
   253→                    raise CircError("DuplicateNameError", f"Duplicate name: {name}",
   254→                                   self.filename, line_num, line.find(name) + 1)
   255→                target.append(Signal(name, msb, lsb))
   256→                existing_names.add(name)
   257→            else:
   258→                if not _is_valid_identifier(part):
   259→                    raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid identifier: {part}",
   260→                                   self.filename, line_num, line.find(part) + 1)
   261→                if part in existing_names:
   262→                    raise CircError("DuplicateNameError", f"Duplicate name: {part}",
   263→                                   self.filename, line_num, line.find(part) + 1)
   264→                target.append(Signal(part, 0, 0))
   265→                existing_names.add(part)
   266→
   267→    def _parse_assignment(self, line: str, circuit: Circuit, line_num: int, raw_line: str):
   268→        eq_pos = line.find('=')
   269→        lhs = line[:eq_pos].strip()
   270→        rhs = line[eq_pos + 1:].strip()
   271→        if not lhs:
   272→            raise CircError("CircParseError", "Missing left-hand side in assignment", self.filename, line_num, 1)
   273→        if not re.match(r'^([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)$', lhs):
   274→            raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid left-hand side: {lhs}", self.filename, line_num, 1)
   275→        rhs_expr, _ = self._parse_expression(rhs, line_num, eq_pos + 2)
   276→        circuit.assignments.append(Assignment(lhs, rhs_expr, line_num, raw_line.find(lhs) + 1))
   277→
   278→    def _parse_expression(self, text: str, line_num: int, start_col: int) -> Tuple[object, int]:
   279→        text = text.strip()
   280→        if not text:
   281→            raise CircError("CircParseError", "Empty expression", self.filename, line_num, start_col)
   282→        if text.startswith('{'):
   283→            return self._parse_concatenation(text, line_num, start_col)
   284→        expr, consumed = self._parse_primary_expr(text, line_num, start_col)
   285→        remaining = text[consumed:].strip()
   286→        while remaining.startswith('['):
   287→            bracket_end = remaining.find(']')
   288→            if bracket_end == -1:
   289→                raise CircError("CircParseError", "Unmatched bracket in index/slice", self.filename, line_num, start_col + consumed)
   290→            bracket_content = remaining[1:bracket_end]
   291→            if ':' in bracket_content:
   292→                parts = bracket_content.split(':')
   293→                if len(parts) != 2:
   294→                    raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid slice: {bracket_content}", self.filename, line_num, start_col + consumed)
   295→                try:
   296→                    hi, lo = int(parts[0].strip()), int(parts[1].strip())
   297→                except ValueError:
   298→                    raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid slice indices: {bracket_content}", self.filename, line_num, start_col + consumed)
   299→                expr = Slice(expr, hi, lo)
   300→            else:
   301→                try:
   302→                    idx = int(bracket_content.strip())
   303→                except ValueError:
   304→                    raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid index: {bracket_content}", self.filename, line_num, start_col + consumed)
   305→                expr = Index(expr, idx)
   306→            consumed += bracket_end + 1
   307→            remaining = text[consumed:].strip()
   308→        return expr, consumed
   309→
   310→    def _try_match_literal(self, text: str):
   311→        m = re.match(r"^(\d+'[bhd])", text, re.IGNORECASE)
   312→        if m:
   313→            prefix = m.group(1).lower()
   314→            rest = text[len(m.group(1)):]
   315→            if prefix.endswith('b'):
   316→                digits_m = re.match(r'^([01_]+)', rest)
   317→            elif prefix.endswith('h'):
   318→                digits_m = re.match(r'^([0-9a-fA-F_]+)', rest)
   319→            else:
   320→                digits_m = re.match(r'^([0-9_]+)', rest)
   321→            if digits_m:
   322→                full = m.group(1) + digits_m.group(1)
   323→                end_pos = len(full)
   324→                if end_pos >= len(text) or not text[end_pos].isalnum() and text[end_pos] != '_':
   325→                    return full
   326→            return None
   327→        if text.lower().startswith('0b') and len(text) > 2:
   328→            m = re.match(r'^(0b[01_]+)', text, re.IGNORECASE)
   329→            if m:
   330→                end_pos = len(m.group(1))
   331→                if end_pos >= len(text) or not text[end_pos].isalnum() and text[end_pos] != '_':
   332→                    return m.group(1)
   333→            return None
   334→        if text.lower().startswith('0x') and len(text) > 2:
   335→            m = re.match(r'^(0x[0-9a-fA-F_]+)', text, re.IGNORECASE)
   336→            if m:
   337→                end_pos = len(m.group(1))
   338→                if end_pos >= len(text) or not text[end_pos].isalnum() and text[end_pos] != '_':
   339→                    return m.group(1)
   340→            return None
   341→        if text and text[0] in ('0', '1'):
   342→            if len(text) == 1 or not text[1].isalnum() and text[1] != '_':
   343→                return text[0]
   344→        return None
   345→
   346→    def _parse_primary_expr(self, text: str, line_num: int, start_col: int) -> Tuple[object, int]:
   347→        text = text.strip()
   348→        if not text:
   349→            raise CircError("CircParseError", "Empty expression", self.filename, line_num, start_col)
   350→        if text.startswith('{'):
   351→            return self._parse_concatenation(text, line_num, start_col)
   352→        if text.startswith('('):
   353→            depth, i = 1, 1
   354→            while i < len(text) and depth > 0:
   355→                if text[i] == '(':
   356→                    depth += 1
   357→                elif text[i] == ')':
   358→                    depth -= 1
   359→                i += 1
   360→            if depth != 0:
   361→                raise CircError("CircParseError", "Unmatched parenthesis", self.filename, line_num, start_col)
   362→            expr, _ = self._parse_expression(text[1:i-1], line_num, start_col + 1)
   363→            return expr, i
   364→        lit_text = self._try_match_literal(text)
   365→        if lit_text is not None:
   366→            value, width = parse_literal(lit_text, self.filename, line_num, start_col)
   367→            return Literal(value, width), len(lit_text)
   368→        call_match = re.match(r'^([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)\s*\(', text)
   369→        if call_match:
   370→            op_name = call_match.group(1).upper()
   371→            op_start = call_match.end()
   372→            paren_count, i = 1, op_start
   373→            while i < len(text) and paren_count > 0:
   374→                if text[i] == '(':
   375→                    paren_count += 1
   376→                elif text[i] == ')':
   377→                    paren_count -= 1
   378→                i += 1
   379→            if paren_count != 0:
   380→                raise CircError("CircParseError", "Unmatched parenthesis in expression", self.filename, line_num, start_col)
   381→            args = self._parse_arguments(text[op_start:i-1], line_num, start_col + op_start)
   382→            return Call(op_name, args), i
   383→        id_match = re.match(r'^([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)', text)
   384→        if id_match:
   385→            name = id_match.group(1)
   386→            return Identifier(name), len(name)
   387→        raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid expression: {text[:50]}", self.filename, line_num, start_col)
   388→
   389→    def _parse_concatenation(self, text: str, line_num: int, start_col: int) -> Tuple[Concat, int]:
   390→        if not text.startswith('{'):
   391→            raise CircError("CircParseError", "Expected '{' for concatenation", self.filename, line_num, start_col)
   392→        depth, i = 1, 1
   393→        while i < len(text) and depth > 0:
   394→            if text[i] == '{':
   395→                depth += 1
   396→            elif text[i] == '}':
   397→                depth -= 1
   398→            i += 1
   399→        if depth != 0:
   400→            raise CircError("CircParseError", "Unmatched brace in concatenation", self.filename, line_num, start_col)
   401→        inner = text[1:i-1]
   402→        raw_parts = _split_comma_separated(inner)
   403→        if not raw_parts:
   404→            raise CircError("CircParseError", "Empty concatenation", self.filename, line_num, start_col)
   405→        parts = [self._parse_expression(p, line_num, start_col + 1 + idx)[0] for idx, p in enumerate(raw_parts)]
   406→        return Concat(parts), i
   407→
   408→    def _parse_arguments(self, text: str, line_num: int, start_col: int) -> list:
   409→        text = text.strip()
   410→        if not text:
   411→            return []
   412→        raw_parts = _split_comma_separated(text)
   413→        args = []
   414→        offset = 0
   415→        for raw in raw_parts:
   416→            idx = text.index(raw, offset)
   417→            expr, _ = self._parse_expression(raw, line_num, start_col + idx)
   418→            args.append(expr)
   419→            offset = idx + len(raw)
   420→        return args
   421→

   423→def _validate_bench_expr(expr, filename: str, line_num: int, defined_names: set):
   424→    if isinstance(expr, Literal):
   425→        raise CircError("BenchParseError", "Literals are not allowed in BENCH format", filename, line_num)
   426→    if isinstance(expr, Identifier):
   427→        if expr.name not in defined_names:
   428→            raise CircError("UndefinedNameError", f"Undefined signal: {expr.name}", filename, line_num)
   429→        return
   430→    if isinstance(expr, Call):
   431→        for arg in expr.args:
   432→            _validate_bench_expr(arg, filename, line_num, defined_names)
   433→        return
   434→    if isinstance(expr, Index):
   435→        raise CircError("BenchParseError", "Indexing is not allowed in BENCH format", filename, line_num)
   436→    if isinstance(expr, Slice):
   437→        raise CircError("BenchParseError", "Slicing is not allowed in BENCH format", filename, line_num)
   438→    if isinstance(expr, Concat):
   439→        raise CircError("BenchParseError", "Concatenation is not allowed in BENCH format", filename, line_num)
   440→
