   442→def parse_json_circuit(content: str, filename: str) -> Circuit:
   443→    try:
   444→        data = json.loads(content)
   445→    except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
   446→        raise CircError("JsonParseError", f"Invalid JSON: {e.msg}", filename, e.lineno, e.colno)
   447→
   448→    if not isinstance(data, dict):
   449→        raise CircError("JsonSchemaError", "JSON root must be an object", filename)
   450→    if data.get("format_version") != 1:
   451→        raise CircError("JsonSchemaError", f"Unsupported format_version: {data.get('format_version')}, expected 1", filename)
   452→
   453→    for field_name in ("inputs", "outputs", "wires", "assignments"):
   454→        if field_name not in data:
   455→            raise CircError("JsonSchemaError", f"Missing required field: {field_name}", filename)
   456→        if not isinstance(data[field_name], list):
   457→            raise CircError("JsonSchemaError", f"Field '{field_name}' must be an array", filename)
   458→
   459→    circuit = Circuit()
   460→    all_names = set()
   461→
   462→    def parse_port(port_data, target_list, kind):
   463→        if not isinstance(port_data, dict):
   464→            raise CircError("JsonSchemaError", f"{kind} must be an object", filename)
   465→        if "name" not in port_data:
   466→            raise CircError("JsonSchemaError", f"{kind} missing required field: name", filename)
   467→        name = port_data["name"]
   468→        if not isinstance(name, str):
   469→            raise CircError("JsonSchemaError", f"{kind} name must be a string", filename)
   470→        if not re.match(r'^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*$', name):
   471→            raise CircError("JsonSchemaError", f"Invalid identifier: {name}", filename)
   472→        msb = port_data.get("msb", 0)
   473→        lsb = port_data.get("lsb", 0)
   474→        if not isinstance(msb, int) or not isinstance(lsb, int):
   475→            raise CircError("JsonSchemaError", f"{kind} msb and lsb must be integers", filename)
   476→        if msb < lsb:
   477→            raise CircError("JsonSchemaError", f"{kind} msb ({msb}) must be >= lsb ({lsb})", filename)
   478→        if lsb < 0:
   479→            raise CircError("JsonSchemaError", f"{kind} lsb ({lsb}) must be >= 0", filename)
   480→        if name in all_names:
   481→            raise CircError("DuplicateNameError", f"Duplicate name: {name}", filename)
   482→        all_names.add(name)
   483→        target_list.append(Signal(name, msb, lsb))
   484→
   485→    for port in data["inputs"]:
   486→        parse_port(port, circuit.inputs, "Input port")
   487→    for port in data["outputs"]:
   488→        parse_port(port, circuit.outputs, "Output port")
   489→    for port in data["wires"]:
   490→        parse_port(port, circuit.wires, "Wire port")
   491→
   492→    assigned_lhs = set()
   493→    for i, asn_data in enumerate(data["assignments"]):
   494→        if not isinstance(asn_data, dict):
   495→            raise CircError("JsonSchemaError", f"Assignment {i} must be an object", filename)
   496→        if "lhs" not in asn_data:
   497→            raise CircError("JsonSchemaError", f"Assignment {i} missing required field: lhs", filename)
   498→        if "rhs" not in asn_data:
   499→            raise CircError("JsonSchemaError", f"Assignment {i} missing required field: rhs", filename)
   500→        lhs = asn_data["lhs"]
   501→        rhs_str = asn_data["rhs"]
   502→        if not isinstance(lhs, str):
   503→            raise CircError("JsonSchemaError", f"Assignment {i} lhs must be a string", filename)
   504→        if not isinstance(rhs_str, str):
   505→            raise CircError("JsonSchemaError", f"Assignment {i} rhs must be a string", filename)
   506→        if lhs not in all_names:
   507→            raise CircError("UndefinedNameError", f"Undefined signal: {lhs}", filename)
   508→        if lhs in assigned_lhs:
   509→            raise CircError("RedefinitionError", f"Signal assigned multiple times: {lhs}", filename)
   510→        assigned_lhs.add(lhs)
   511→
   512→        parser = CircParser("", filename)
   513→        try:
   514→            rhs_expr, _ = parser._parse_expression(rhs_str, 1, 1)
   515→        except CircError as e:
   516→            raise CircError(e.error_type, e.message, filename, 1, e.col)
   517→        circuit.assignments.append(Assignment(lhs, rhs_expr, 1, 1))
   518→
   519→    for sig in circuit.outputs + circuit.wires:
   520→        if sig.name not in assigned_lhs:
   521→            kind = "Output" if sig in circuit.outputs else "Wire"
   522→            raise CircError("UnassignedSignalError", f"{kind} not assigned: {sig.name}", filename)
   523→
   524→    return circuit

   527→def parse_bench_circuit(content: str, filename: str) -> Circuit:
   528→    circuit = Circuit()
   529→    all_names = set()
   530→    defined_wires = set()
   531→    output_refs = set()
   532→    assignments_list = []
   533→
   534→    lines = content.split('\n')
   535→    for line_num, line in enumerate(lines, 1):
   536→        if '#' in line:
   537→            line = line[:line.index('#')]
   538→        line = line.strip()
   539→        if not line:
   540→            continue
   541→
   542→        if '[' in line or ']' in line:
   543→            raise CircError("BenchParseError", "BENCH format does not support vector signals (brackets not allowed)", filename, line_num)
   544→
   545→        input_match = re.match(r'^INPUT\s*\(\s*([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)\s*\)$', line, re.IGNORECASE)
   546→        if input_match:
   547→            name = input_match.group(1)
   548→            if name in all_names:
   549→                raise CircError("RedefinitionError", f"Signal defined multiple times: {name}", filename, line_num)
   550→            all_names.add(name)
   551→            circuit.inputs.append(Signal(name, 0, 0))
   552→            continue
   553→
   554→        output_match = re.match(r'^OUTPUT\s*\(\s*([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)\s*\)$', line, re.IGNORECASE)
   555→        if output_match:
   556→            name = output_match.group(1)
   557→            if name in output_refs:
   558→                raise CircError("RedefinitionError", f"OUTPUT declared multiple times: {name}", filename, line_num)
   559→            output_refs.add(name)
   560→            circuit.outputs.append(Signal(name, 0, 0))
   561→            continue
   562→
   563→        assign_match = re.match(r'^([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)\s*=\s*(.+)$', line)
   564→        if assign_match:
   565→            lhs = assign_match.group(1)
   566→            rhs_str = assign_match.group(2).strip()
   567→            if lhs in defined_wires:
   568→                raise CircError("RedefinitionError", f"Signal assigned multiple times: {lhs}", filename, line_num)
   569→            if lhs in all_names and lhs not in output_refs:
   570→                raise CircError("InputAssignmentError", f"Cannot assign to input: {lhs}", filename, line_num)

1→"""Circuit validation using visitor pattern."""
     2→
     3→from ast_nodes import Circuit, Literal, Identifier, Index, Slice, Concat, Call, ExprVisitor, accept
     4→from errors import CircError
     5→from operators import OPERATOR_ARITY, operator_width, _WIDTH_PROPAGATE, _SELECT, _REDUCE
     6→
     7→
     8→class Validator:
     9→    def __init__(self, circuit: Circuit, filename: str):
    10→        self.circuit = circuit
    11→        self.filename = filename
    12→        self.signal_types = {sig.name: 'input' for sig in circuit.inputs}
    13→        self.signal_types.update({sig.name: 'output' for sig in circuit.outputs})
    14→        self.signal_types.update({sig.name: 'wire' for sig in circuit.wires})
    15→        self.signal_info = {sig.name: sig for sig in circuit.inputs + circuit.outputs + circuit.wires}
    16→        self._all_names = circuit.get_all_names()
    17→        self._signal_widths = {name: sig.width for name, sig in self.signal_info.items()}
    18→        self._input_names = {sig.name for sig in circuit.inputs}
    19→
    20→    def validate(self):
    21→        self._check_assignments()
    22→        self._check_cycles()
    23→
    24→    def _check_assignments(self):
    25→        assigned = set()
    26→        for asn in self.circuit.assignments:
    27→            if asn.lhs not in self._all_names:
    28→                raise CircError("UndefinedNameError", f"Undefined signal: {asn.lhs}", self.filename, asn.line, asn.col)
    29→            if self.signal_types.get(asn.lhs) == 'input':
    30→                raise CircError("InputAssignmentError", f"Cannot assign to input: {asn.lhs}", self.filename, asn.line, asn.col)
    31→            if asn.lhs in assigned:
    32→                raise CircError("MultipleAssignmentError", f"Signal assigned multiple times: {asn.lhs}", self.filename, asn.line, asn.col)
    33→            assigned.add(asn.lhs)
    34→            self._validate_expr(asn.rhs, asn.line)
    35→
    36→        for sig in self.circuit.outputs + self.circuit.wires:
    37→            if sig.name not in assigned:
    38→                if sig.name in self._input_names and self.signal_types.get(sig.name) == 'output':
    39→                    continue
    40→                kind = "Output" if self.signal_types.get(sig.name) == 'output' else "Wire"
    41→                raise CircError("UnassignedSignalError", f"{kind} not assigned: {sig.name}", self.filename)
    42→
    43→    def _validate_expr(self, expr, line_num: int) -> int:
    44→        return accept(expr, _ValidateVisitor(self, line_num))
    45→
    46→
    47→class _ValidateVisitor(ExprVisitor):
    48→    def __init__(self, validator: Validator, line_num: int):
    49→        self.validator = validator
    50→        self.line_num = line_num
    51→
    52→    def visit_literal(self, expr: Literal):
    53→        return expr.width
    54→
    55→    def visit_identifier(self, expr: Identifier):
    56→        if expr.name not in self.validator._all_names:
    57→            raise CircError("UndefinedNameError", f"Undefined signal: {expr.name}", self.validator.filename, self.line_num)
    58→        return self.validator._signal_widths[expr.name]
    59→
    60→    def visit_index(self, expr: Index):
    61→        base_width = self.validator._validate_expr(expr.expr, self.line_num)
    62→        self._check_index_bounds(expr, base_width)
    63→        return 1
    64→
    65→    def visit_slice(self, expr: Slice):
    66→        base_width = self.validator._validate_expr(expr.expr, self.line_num)
    67→        if expr.hi < expr.lo:
    68→            raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid slice: hi ({expr.hi}) < lo ({expr.lo})",
    69→                           self.validator.filename, self.line_num)
    70→        self._check_slice_bounds(expr, base_width)
    71→        return expr.hi - expr.lo + 1
    72→
    73→    def visit_concat(self, expr: Concat):
    74→        return sum(self.validator._validate_expr(p, self.line_num) for p in expr.parts)
    75→
    76→    def visit_call(self, expr: Call):
    77→        self._check_arity(expr)
    78→        arg_widths = [self.validator._validate_expr(arg, self.line_num) for arg in expr.args]
    79→        self._check_widths(expr, arg_widths)
    80→        return operator_width(expr.operator, arg_widths)
    81→
    82→    def _check_index_bounds(self, expr: Index, base_width: int):
    83→        sig = self.validator.signal_info.get(expr.expr.name) if isinstance(expr.expr, Identifier) else None
    84→        if sig:
    85→            if expr.index < sig.lsb or expr.index > sig.msb:
    86→                raise CircError("IndexOutOfBoundsError",
    87→                               f"Index {expr.index} out of bounds for signal {sig.name}[{sig.msb}:{sig.lsb}]",
    88→                               self.validator.filename, self.line_num)
    89→        elif expr.index < 0 or expr.index >= base_width:
    90→            raise CircError("IndexOutOfBoundsError",
    91→                           f"Index {expr.index} out of bounds for expression of width {base_width}",
    92→                           self.validator.filename, self.line_num)
    93→
    94→    def _check_slice_bounds(self, expr: Slice, base_width: int):
    95→        sig = self.validator.signal_info.get(expr.expr.name) if isinstance(expr.expr, Identifier) else None
    96→        if sig:
    97→            if expr.lo < sig.lsb or expr.hi > sig.msb:
    98→                raise CircError("IndexOutOfBoundsError",
    99→                               f"Slice [{expr.hi}:{expr.lo}] out of bounds for signal {sig.name}[{sig.msb}:{sig.lsb}]",
   100→                               self.validator.filename, self.line_num)
   101→        elif expr.lo < 0 or expr.hi >= base_width:
   102→            raise CircError("IndexOutOfBoundsError",
   103→                           f"Slice [{expr.hi}:{expr.lo}] out of bounds for expression of width {base_width}",
   104→                           self.validator.filename, self.line_num)
   105→
   106→    def _check_arity(self, call: Call):
   107→        op = call.operator
   108→        num_args = len(call.args)
   109→        if op not in OPERATOR_ARITY:
   110→            raise CircError("UndefinedNameError", f"Unknown operator: {op}", self.validator.filename, self.line_num)
   111→        min_args, max_args = OPERATOR_ARITY[op]
   112→        if num_args < min_args or (max_args and num_args > max_args):
   113→            if max_args == min_args:
   114→                req = f"exactly {min_args}"
   115→            elif max_args is None:
   116→                req = f"at least {min_args}"
   117→            else:
   118→                req = f"between {min_args} and {max_args}"
   119→            plural = "s" if min_args > 1 or (max_args and max_args > 1) else ""
   120→            raise CircError("ArityError", f"Operator {op} requires {req} argument{plural}, got {num_args}",
   121→                           self.validator.filename, self.line_num)
   122→
   123→    def _check_widths(self, call: Call, arg_widths: list):
   124→        op = call.operator
   125→        if op in _WIDTH_PROPAGATE:
   126→            for i, w in enumerate(arg_widths[1:], 1):
   127→                if w != arg_widths[0]:
   128→                    raise CircError("WidthMismatchError",
   129→                                   f"Width mismatch in {op}: operand 0 has width {arg_widths[0]}, operand {i} has width {w}",
   130→                                   self.validator.filename, self.line_num)
   131→        elif op in _SELECT:
   132→            if arg_widths[0] != 1:
   133→                raise CircError("WidthMismatchError",
   134→                               f"Selector in {op} must have width 1, got width {arg_widths[0]}",
   135→                               self.validator.filename, self.line_num)
   136→            if arg_widths[1] != arg_widths[2]:
   137→                raise CircError("WidthMismatchError",
   138→                               f"Width mismatch in {op}: operand 1 has width {arg_widths[1]}, operand 2 has width {arg_widths[2]}",
   139→                               self.validator.filename, self.line_num)
   140→        elif op in _REDUCE:
   141→            pass
   142→        elif op == 'EQ':
   143→            if arg_widths[0] != arg_widths[1]:
   144→                raise CircError("WidthMismatchError",
   145→                               f"Width mismatch in EQ: operand 0 has width {arg_widths[0]}, operand 1 has width {arg_widths[1]}",
   146→                               self.validator.filename, self.line_num)
